races.name: BetMGM Burradon Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Andab
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three Group races on turf before Listed win at Dundalk (7f, Polytrack) last October; went close in Local Group 2 in Qatar (1m) in February and this Irish challenger holds leading claims under a 3lb penalty.
runners.comment: Listed winner at Dundalk last October and holds leading claims under a 3lb penalty

runners.name: Timeforshowcasing
runners.spotlight: Three wins from her five 2yo starts, including on Polytrack (sole run on AW) and a fillies' series final at Goodwood (7f) in September when last seen; not beaten far when fifth in the Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m) on penultimate outing and she could be involved on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Three wins from her five 2yo starts and she could be involved on this reappearance

runners.name: New Monarch
runners.spotlight: Progressive in defeat on turf across his four 2yo starts and he reappeared with a novice win at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) 16 days ago, when odds-on; further improvement is necessary if he's to follow up today, but that can't be ruled out and there's every chance 1m will be fine; yard in good form.
runners.comment: Won Kempton novice recently; further improvement needed today but that can't be ruled out

runners.name: Alarming
runners.spotlight: Won Kempton novice (7f, Polytrack) last October on her third start and she's been third in Listed races at Saint-Cloud on both starts since (7f/1m, heavy), albeit without seriously threatening on either occasion; in good hands to continue to progress and she might not be far away.
runners.comment: Third in two Listed races in France and she might not be far away

runners.name: Jel Pepper
runners.spotlight: Third in the Group 2 July Stakes at Newmarket (6f) last July and tapped back into that promise, and more, to win valuable sales race on the other course there (7f) in October; there can be optimism he will be suited by the step up to 1m and he could be in the mix if fully tuned up on his return.
runners.comment: Won valuable 7f sales race at Newmarket last October; could be in the mix on his return

runners.name: Billecart
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last July on sole 2yo start and he easily justified 1-6 odds in a four-runner novice over this C&D four weeks ago; today is a much tougher test of his credentials but this 2,000 Guineas entry could have plenty more to offer for his top northern yard.
runners.comment: Easily won C&D novice last month; could have plenty more to offer for top northern yard

runners.name: Padraig Dawn
runners.spotlight: Recovered from slow start to win at 11-5 on debut at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) in February, keeping on well and shaping as though he'd be suited by 1m; others have far greater substance to their form but he's very unexposed and his dam Termagent was a Group 1 winner; not discounted.
runners.comment: 7f debut win at Southwell in February; others have much stronger form but dam won Group 1

races.name: BetMGM All-Weather 3 Year Old Championships Handicap

runners.name: Al Najashi
runners.spotlight: 4-4 since fitted with a tongue tie and switched to handicaps; now a whopping 32lb higher than when his winning sequence began and he is tackling much stronger opposition this afternoon but he barely broke sweat last time and his ceiling is yet to be established.
runners.comment: 4-4 in handicaps; risen 32lb but quite possible there's a fair bit more to come

runners.name: Sparksmith
runners.spotlight: He has improved his RPR with each of his four starts, the latest when beaten a nose at Kempton last month on handicap debut (6f); up 2lb but has a promising 7lb claimer booked; can do better.
runners.comment: Improved his RPR with each of his four starts; up in class but promising claimer booked

runners.name: Silent Strike
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite for all three starts (6f), confirming the promise of his debut second when easily scoring at Kempton in February; similar form when second over C&D four weeks ago, giving best only to a promising filly; still has potential and while this opening mark wouldn't look an obvious gift, his trainer/jockey have combined to win both runnings of this race since it became a handicap and they also won the 2023 renewal.
runners.comment: Promising sprinter and represents the trainer/jockey that have won the last three runnings

runners.name: Ten Carat Harry
runners.spotlight: His winning spree came to an end but he emerged with plenty of credit following his third in a 7f Listed event at Lingfield five weeks ago; another personal best will be required if he's to win giving weight away all round but he continues to thrive on racing.
runners.comment: Lost unbeaten AW record last time but still produced a fine effort in Listed company

runners.name: Cotai Lights
runners.spotlight: 8lb better off with Silent Strike for 3l on their C&D run last month; solid third at Naas on recent handicap debut (7f, soft); drop back in trip not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Can win handicaps off this mark but dropping back to 6f isn't sure to suit

runners.name: Three Non Blondes
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at Chelmsford in December (6f) before chasing home Riley Rocks (winner off a BHA mark of 75 since) over C&D on New Year's Day; made all for a smooth success in a 7f handicap at Wolverhampton nine weeks ago; up 8lb but still has potential.
runners.comment: Three good AW runs this winter; up 8lb for latest win but she still has some potential

runners.name: One And Gone
runners.spotlight: Ran well but finished behind Ten Carat Harry in his first three handicaps and he was fourth to Saffron Dandy at Lingfield last time; needs to find extra to win.
runners.comment: Finished behind Ten Carat Harry three times this winter and behind Saffron Dandy latest

runners.name: Monarchs Gold
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as a 2yo; creditable fifth to Ten Carat Harry over C&D in January (handicap debut) before bumping into one at Southwell three weeks ago; cheekpieces need to prompt improvement.
runners.comment: Two creditable handicap runs this year but he needs the new headgear to give him a lift

runners.name: Jungle Ruler
runners.spotlight: Debut win for Joseph O'Brien at Dundalk (7f) last October; ready win at Southwell on stable debut (6f) in January and ran well under a double penalty at Lingfield two weeks later; finished behind two of today's rivals when fourth at Southwell three weeks ago; needs to raise his game but that is possible.
runners.comment: Only 4th on last month's handicap debut; still low mileage and this stiff 6f should suit

runners.name: Saffron Dandy
runners.spotlight: Won her first two starts this year, over 6f at Wolverhampton and at Lingfield; came up shy in her hat-trick bid at Southwell three weeks ago (met some trouble) but could be suited by today's stiffer test.
runners.comment: Won her first two handicaps before meeting some trouble latest; stiff 6f may help

runners.name: Eternal Solace
runners.spotlight: Two wins in maiden/novice company; has shown promise in handicaps, including her Kempton third last month when she couldn't quite sustain the big move she made in the penultimate furlong; has a better chance than her odds may imply; stable also runs Al Najashi.
runners.comment: Not won a handicap but the move she made in the penultimate furlong last time was striking

runners.name: Justmyluck
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 5f handicap here in January but only fifth of seven at Wolverhampton last time; others are open to greater progress.
runners.comment: 5f course winner in January (Class 5); only fifth last time and looks vulnerable today

runners.name: Northern Empire
runners.spotlight: Promise in his first two starts (7f/1m) and took a step forward when winning a good novice at Lingfield in January (7f), making all and finding plenty; should be quick enough for 6f and connections should have a good marker as Lord Harcourt, who also runs in these colours, has form that ties in with several of the runners.
runners.comment: Good 7f win in January; drops in trip for handicap debut and retains significant potential

runners.name: Rogue Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Three fair efforts this year but without suggesting he was well enough handicapped for a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Three fair efforts this year but others look to be better treated

races.name: BetMGM Fillies' And Mares' Championships Handicap

runners.name: Stateira
runners.spotlight: 2-2 at Newcastle, recording wide-margin handicap wins over 1m in November and this trip in the trial in January; the form of the latest win is unconvincing, however, and she had been beaten by Bridget's View at Southwell on her penultimate start; has to be respected but this will require another step forward.
runners.comment: Two Newcastle visits have yielded two wide-margin wins; up 10lb; needs to progress again

runners.name: Ziggys Queen
runners.spotlight: Coped well with the rise in class at Lingfield five weeks ago, finishing with a flourish and nabbing Powdering close home; that was her first attempt at 7f, and in a hood, and she could have more to offer at the trip.
runners.comment: Improved for 7th furlong when nabbing Powdering at Lingfield five weeks ago; considered

runners.name: Bridgets View
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner as a 2yo; returned from a seven-month absence to beat Stateira by 4.25l at Southwell in December (1m) and she is 1lb better off with that rival today; not beaten far in a blanket finish at Lingfield five weeks ago (7f; stable debut) and this stiffer test should suit; one to consider with a tongue tie now fitted.
runners.comment: Of interest on her best form and latest 5th (stable debut) was respectable; each-way shout

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Four wins and a second from her five AW runs at Dundalk, including 7f and 1m wins in her two runs in 2026; back against her own sex today and likely to go close.
runners.comment: Good record on Dundalk's AW track; back against females this time; high on the list
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Callianassa
runners.spotlight: Her three course wins have come over a mile, the latest when beating Dosman (runs in the 3.00) in February; slightly disappointing here four weeks ago but she is back against her own sex and not handicapped out of things.
runners.comment: Three 1m wins here, latest off 2lb lower; not at best last month; back against own sex now

runners.name: Paris Babe
runners.spotlight: Two wins in France last year, including on the AW at Deauville (6.5f); two creditable efforts this year before changing hands for 32,000euros in February; thrown into a tough race on her stable debut.
runners.comment: Two wins in France; sold 32,000euros in February; this looks a tough stable debut

runners.name: Brazilian Rose
runners.spotlight: Fifth of 14 in the corresponding race 12 months ago when rated 6lb lower; four wins at Southwell this year, the latest over 6f three weeks ago, but that shorter trip probably suits her better.
runners.comment: Four Southwell wins this year (6f/7f); up in weights and would be more appealing over 6f

runners.name: Indigo Dream
runners.spotlight: Didn't have to improve to win a 7f maiden at Dundalk in December; 5.25l behind Stateira when third over C&D 11 weeks ago; a career best will be required today.
runners.comment: Didn't have to improve to win a maiden in December; no match for Stateira last time

runners.name: Fouette
runners.spotlight: Impressed at Southwell in October (after a 425-day layoff) and defied market weakness in a Chelmsford handicap three weeks later (both 7f); returns to handicap company off a 15lb higher mark than for the second win but that is a result of two fine runs in Listed company since; raced freely under a more prominent ride than usual at Deauville last time; could benefit from a return to hold-up tactics and she isn't fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Two 7f AW handicap wins in October; good runs in Listed company since; contender

runners.name: Shallow
runners.spotlight: Ran a cracker when fourth at York's Ebor meeting (7f) and now rated 6lb lower; she hasn't been at her best yet this year though and a full revival is required.
runners.comment: Has been disappointing since York fourth last August; lowly mark but needs a revival

runners.name: Powdering
runners.spotlight: She has had plenty of racing but she retains plenty of ability and went agonisingly close at Lingfield five weeks ago, pipped at the post by Ziggy's Queen; should give it another good go.
runners.comment: Tough 6yo who just missed out at Lingfield in February; can feature once again

races.name: BetMGM All-Weather Championships Mile Handicap

runners.name: Storm Star
runners.spotlight: He was completing a C&D hat-trick when winning the corresponding race 12 months ago off a 3lb lower mark; not at his best in two runs this year (following a gelding operation) and he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 3lb lower; doesn't arrive in the same form this time around

runners.name: The Lost King
runners.spotlight: The form of his Kempton win (7f) in January could hardly have worked out better and he raised his game again to win the Lincoln Trial at Wolverhampton four weeks ago; still not fully exposed and he is a leading contender up 5lb.
runners.comment: 2-3 this year and still improving; 5lb rise shouldn't prevent another big run

runners.name: Chancellor
runners.spotlight: He has improved for gelding/wind operations, winning a 7f handicap at Kempton and a 1m Listed event at Southwell this winter; narrowly denied in the Winter Derby six weeks ago (not a vintage renewal); the demands of this race should be more suitable and he still has Pattern-race aspirations; he'll need to be a Group horse to give the weight away all round but he is an each-way contender at the very least; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Enjoying a good winter; cheekpieces now added; each-way shout despite top weight

runners.name: Witch Hunter
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of the trial over C&D in January and a 3lb rise isn't beyond him on that evidence; two lesser efforts have followed (sustained a small wound to his right-hind fetlock last time) but his fine record at Newcastle means he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Two wins and two placed efforts from five Newcastle visits; big run on the cards

runners.name: First Principle
runners.spotlight: He has a good record on AW, winning three times and runner-up on his other two attempts; got the better of Dosman at Wolverhampton two weeks ago but he looks held by The Lost King on their Lincoln Trial form.
runners.comment: Good record on AW and successful two weeks ago; looks held by The Lost King though

runners.name: Shafdar
runners.spotlight: Promising stable debut at Kempton in January and he easily won a 1m maiden at Chelmsford in early February; only second to a stablemate back at Chelmsford for his handicap debut three weeks ago; can win more races but probably not this one.
runners.comment: Effectively 5lb higher than when beaten in a Class 4 last month; tough task

runners.name: Apiarist
runners.spotlight: Finished a good third in this race 12 months ago when rated 2lb lower; arrives in good form, having beaten Popmaster at Lingfield (1m) before a good third at Kempton (7f) 16 days ago; chance if stall 1 proves no hindrance.
runners.comment: 3rd in this race last year off 2lb lower; arrives in good order and he can't be discounted

runners.name: Popmaster
runners.spotlight: Arrives in fine form, beating The Lost King at Kempton in February and runner-up on his two runs since; sneaking back up the weights and he was only fifth in this race 12 months ago off today's mark.
runners.comment: Arrives in good form but only fifth off this mark in the 2025 renewal; likely vulnerable

runners.name: Dosman
runners.spotlight: Coaxed back to form by his new trainer, twice placed over C&D before winning at Southwell in February; good second behind First Principle two weeks ago; another 2lb rise tempers enthusiasm slightly.
runners.comment: Back in form for new yard & fine with prevailing conditions; others perhaps better treated

runners.name: Tyrrhenian Sea
runners.spotlight: As reliable as they come on AW, winning five of his 16 starts in this sphere and second or third on a further nine occasions; has a rare handicap run today but he looks held by Chancellor on their Southwell clash in February (3lb better off for a length but the winner had a bit to spare).
runners.comment: Reliable on AW but suspicion a few of these will have his measure at the weights

runners.name: Blue Rc
runners.spotlight: The winner of four of his eight starts and he had a bit to spare when rewarding odds-on favourite backers at Southwell on New Year's Day; unlikely this mark will prove beyond him in time; solid candidate.
runners.comment: Won four of his eight starts; Southwell win in January was decisive; still improving

runners.name: Rogue Encore
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 4 over C&D in September; unplaced in three runs this year, the latest here last week, and this looks a tough race in which to try to rebound.
runners.comment: C&D winner in September; unplaced in three runs this year; enough to prove for now

runners.name: Nikovo
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner, the latest when recording a last-gasp success over 7f four weeks ago; 2lb higher mark to contend with in a stronger race; bettering last season's sixth would be a solid effort.
runners.comment: Conditions suit & arrives on back of a win; bettering last year's 6th would be a good run

runners.name: Flight Plan
runners.spotlight: Quiet in Dubai in January but he returned to Britain with a good fourth at Lingfield (1m2f) five weeks ago; now 6lb lower than when winning the Thirsk Hunt Cup last May and he has the ability to feature.
runners.comment: Returning to 1m in his favour and he lurks on a dangerous mark; not ruled out at a price
runners.owner: Fine Clarets Racing Tp Watson & C Wood

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Sarab Star
runners.spotlight: Unexposed and progressive handicapper who has two wins and two seconds to his name from four AW appearances; lost little in defeat at Kempton 16 days ago when chasing home a smart rival who was winning the race for the fourth time; a stiff 6f should be fine but stall 1 might not be beneficial given the forecast of a brisk crosswind; stable also runs Ferrous.
runners.comment: Progressive at 7f; stiff 6f should be fine; stall 1 perhaps not ideal given forecast wind

runners.name: Valiant Force
runners.spotlight: Sprung a huge shock in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes at Royal Ascot; all five wins since have come in small fields at Dundalk; has run well in handicaps off high marks at that track but others are more appealing in today's field; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Five AW wins in small fields at Dundalk; blinkered for the first time; others appeal more
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: City House
runners.spotlight: Often threatened to be high class on AW but it was on turf in Bahrain where he leapt to prominence, enjoying a fine time in early 2025; improving run by run for new stable but he was put in his place by Sir Les Patterson at Dundalk six weeks ago and an 8lb turnaround may be insufficient to turn the tables.
runners.comment: Talented on his day and latest Dundalk run was his best for new connections

runners.name: Berkshire Whisper
runners.spotlight: He was completing a hat-trick when landing the 3yo sprint on this card 12 months ago, a race where he enjoyed a clear run while several in behind met trouble; he hasn't looked ahead of the handicapper in three runs this winter but likely this has been the main target and he has been gelded since his latest run.
runners.comment: Won 3yo h'cap on this card in 2025 off 9lb lower; fair form this winter; has been gelded
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Lakers
runners.spotlight: Impressed at Southwell last October when dropped back to 6f; two solid efforts in defeat this year and this stiff test at the trip should be beneficial; shortlisted.
runners.comment: On the up since dropped back to 6f; this test should play to his strengths; shortlisted

runners.name: Marshman
runners.spotlight: Did well in early 2025 on AW, winning a C&D handicap in good style and following up in Listed company at Lingfield; finished third in the corresponding race 12 months ago when rated 5lb higher; running himself back into form this winter, most recently a close third over 7f four weeks ago, and he looks primed to give Clifford Lee a winning return to the saddle.
runners.comment: Classy sprinter; 5lb lower than when 3rd last year; improved race by race this winter
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 2 & Mrs E Burke

runners.name: So Darn Hot
runners.spotlight: Met all sorts of trouble when down the field behind Berkshire Whisper here 12 months ago; beaten in races won by reopposing rivals in all three starts for his new stable this winter; edging down the weights and has a good apprentice booked but others still appeal more.
runners.comment: Fair form for new yard this winter but he'll need a personal best to win this hot contest

runners.name: El Bodon
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins at Kempton this winter and he ran as well as he ever has when a close fourth at Lingfield five weeks ago (behind Ferrous and Lakers); this stiff 6f should suit and he can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Progressing on AW this winter; today's stiff 6f should suit; in the mix again

runners.name: Heathcliff
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; running well in handicaps this winter before a fine second in Listed company at Wolverhampton four weeks ago; the 10lb hike for that will surely leave him vulnerable.
runners.comment: Smart on AW but a 10lb rise for latest Listed second surely leaves him vulnerable

runners.name: Pocklington
runners.spotlight: Rewarded strong market support in game fashion at Southwell on New Year's Day (6f); another solid effort when second of 14 over C&D two weeks later; 2lb higher today but there is a fair chance he hasn't reached his ceiling after just 12 starts.
runners.comment: Two good AW runs in January; still has relatively low mileage; respected once more

runners.name: Wiltshire
runners.spotlight: Smart form in 2024, including over C&D; only ran once in 2025 and he has finished out of the places in all three starts this year but that doesn't tell the whole story as he made some striking late headway into sixth over C&D in January; he has undergone wind surgery since last seen and now sports a tongue tie; looks on a dangerous mark and could be a major player if he can avoid forfeiting too much ground at the start.
runners.comment: Well-handicapped C&D winner; wind op since latest; dangerous, for all the start is a worry

runners.name: Sir Les Patterson
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but talented when trained by Harry Eustace; on the up for his new stable, winning 6f handicaps in fine style last November and in February; could well have more to offer on AW but it will require a mighty performance to defy top weight in this field.
runners.comment: Easy winner of 6f handicaps at Dundalk on his last two starts; this mark demands much more

runners.name: Ferrous
runners.spotlight: Handicap mark not impossible judged on his C&D win last May off 3lb lower; running okay in defeat this winter and it would be no surprise to see him in the thick of the action.
runners.comment: C&D win last May came off 3lb lower; running OK this winter and a big run is on the cards

runners.name: Fivethousandtoone
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, including this race in 2024 off a 2lb higher mark, and he has shaped well on his last two starts; he will need luck if it gets congested up the stands' rail but too much ability to rule out.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 off 2lb higher; will need luck in running but he's still quite appealing

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Easter Classic Middle Distance Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Dukes Command
runners.spotlight: Won at Lingfield (Polytrack) in December on first crack at 1m2f; another big run when third of five in Listed race there (1m2f) next time; bounced back from lesser run when staying on for seventh of 12 at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) recently and he could be in the mix now back up in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed over 1m2f and he stayed on well over 8.6f last time; could be in the mix

runners.name: Regal Ulixes
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 4yo who made it 2-3 in this hood when winning readily at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) in February; kept on from the rear for close sixth of 12 in the Lincoln Trial at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) last month and he's firmly in calculations with the return to 1m2f a major plus.
runners.comment: 1m2f win in February and stayed on well over 8.6f subsequently; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Zryan
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (9.4f) in December and over this C&D in January; below best at Wolverhampton (8.6f) latest and he's running in a higher grade than he's used to, but he has an each-way shout on the pick of his winter form.
runners.comment: Running in a higher grade than he's used to, but each-way shout on pick of his winter form

runners.name: Nolton Cross
runners.spotlight: Six wins from his 23 AW starts, the latest at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) in February; has continued in good form when fourth at Lingfield and Kempton on last two starts, but he may again be vulnerable today.
runners.comment: Has continued in good form since February win but he may again be vulnerable today

runners.name: Parlando
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row in December (1m2f/9.4f) and he didn't enjoy an ideal run through when second to King's Code over C&D in January; another good performance when third at Kempton (1m4f) last month and this shorter trip may well be his optimum; could be thereabouts once more.
runners.comment: Enjoying a good run of form in Britain (including C&D) and could be thereabouts once more

runners.name: Antrim
runners.spotlight: Won C&D novice in October for the Gosdens and followed up on stable debut in C&D handicap; added a dirt handicap win at Meydan in January; well beaten in Meydan Group 2 last time but this front-runner could make a bold bid now back in a handicap.
runners.comment: It was a Group 2 at Meydan when well beaten last time; two C&D wins; could make a bold bid

runners.name: Bragbor
runners.spotlight: Two-time course winner who went close on his stable debut over C&D in November; things didn't go his way when fourth of 13 at Chelmsford (1m2f) ten days later; this very lightly raced 5yo goes well fresh and remains open to further improvement; could be a contender following a break.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 5yo who is a two-time course winner; could be a contender after a break

runners.name: Midnight Lion
runners.spotlight: 24 visits to Newcastle have yielded nine wins, seven over C&D; the latest came on New Year's Day when beating five rivals; fifth of 12 at Southwell since and could better that performance now back here, but there's a suspicion today's contest will prove too competitive.
runners.comment: Nine-time course winner but there's a suspicion today's contest will prove too competitive

runners.name: Gaucher
runners.spotlight: Represents top Irish jumps trainer Willie Mullins; won a Grade 3 hurdle at Tipperary last October and he's posted easy wins on the Dundalk AW on his last two starts (1m4f/10.6f); has a lofty mark to defy now back in a handicap but he may well prove capable in view of his impressive winter form.
runners.comment: Grade 3 hurdle winner; impressive on Dundalk AW on last two starts; lots to like
runners.owner: Mrs Rose Boyd & Mrs Marie J Armstrong

runners.name: Paradias
runners.spotlight: Fourth in this last year on sole previous visit to Newcastle and he's now 3lb higher; went close on first two starts this winter before winning at Lingfield (1m4f) in February; could run well but may prefer a longer trip.
runners.comment: 4th in this last year; 1m4f win at Lingfield latest; could run well but may prefer further

runners.name: The Glen Rovers
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins between July 2024 and February 2025, including here; however, he hasn't been at the top of his game on his three starts since November, which puts him down the pecking order today.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner but hasn't been at top of his game on his three starts since November

runners.name: Teumessias Fox
runners.spotlight: Slow starts have become an unwelcome habit but he caught the eye keeping on for fifth of 13 behind King's Code over C&D in January, when denied a clear run; last of three at Lingfield since in a race that wasn't run to suit; each-way claims if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Tends to start slowly but eyecatching fifth over C&D in January and holds each-way claims

runners.name: Kings Code
runners.spotlight: He's never won off a mark this high and he needs to produce more than he's managed at Kempton and Lingfield on his last three starts; however, before those he won a 13-runner handicap over C&D with something to spare and the return to Newcastle could be in his favour; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Unplaced at Kempton/Lingfield on last three starts but won well over C&D in January

runners.name: Brasil Power
runners.spotlight: Won three times in December (1m/1m4f) and continued in good form, getting back on the scoresheet in a five-runner handicap at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) last month; didn't run badly over 2m at Kempton last Saturday; drops back in trip here and may find today's company to be too competitive.
runners.comment: Four wins since December (1m-1m6f) but he may find today's company too competitive

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Easter Plate Marathon Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Beylerbeyi
runners.spotlight: Won the 2m2f Cesarewitch in October and running really well in defeat on AW over 1m2f/1m3f (including here) this year, most recently second at Lingfield behind Regal Ulixes (declared 4:10); leading player back up in trip.
runners.comment: 2m2f Cesarewitch winner; in good form over 1m2f/1m3f this year; leading player

runners.name: Sax Appeal
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who posted four wins in the second half of last year, including over C&D and at Southwell in December; however, he needs to produce more than he's managed on his last three starts.
runners.comment: 4 wins in the second half of last year but has to produce more than on his last 3 starts

runners.name: Berkshire Sundance
runners.spotlight: In and out on his five runs since returning from an absence in October but he's won three of them, most recently at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) in January; has also won on Tapeta; this steadily progressive 6yo has gone up just 2lb for last time and could again be involved; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: In and out but this steadily progressive 6yo has won three of his last five starts
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Dramatic Star
runners.spotlight: Not quite at his best at Kempton last month but that came after 94 days off and he could improve for it; held up in slowly run race at Kempton in October when 2l fourth on his sole previous crack at 2m, and he may yet be capable of better at this sort of trip; has won here and could be involved.
runners.comment: 2l fourth on sole previous attempt at 2m and he may yet be capable of better

runners.name: Tryfan
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent since finishing second in the Northumberland Vase over C&D last June and well beaten over C&D in January, but back on song when second at Chelmsford (1m6f) in February; capable of a bold show in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: 2nd in Northumberland Vase last June, and also at Chelmsford latest; capable of bold show

runners.name: Barenboim
runners.spotlight: Made all at Kempton (2m, Polytrack) in January and head second to Berkshire Sundance at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) later that month; not quite at the same level at Southwell (2m2f, Tapeta) last month but he failed to settle; Newcastle form figures read 12139 and he has an each-way shout.
runners.comment: Some good form this year and course form figures read 12139; has an each-way shout

runners.name: Artisan Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) in November and back on the scoresheet at Southwell (2m, Tapeta) three weeks ago; those wins were in Class 4 and he's back up in grade today off a career-high mark, but he did it readily last time and a 3lb rise isn't harsh; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Two wins from last three starts; back up in grade today but he did it readily last time

runners.name: Tribal Star
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this last year and also ran well over C&D when fourth of 20 in the Northumberland Plate last June; he's been running pretty well at Dundalk this year and could build on those efforts now back here.
runners.comment: Second in this last year & fourth of 20 in the Northumberland Plate last June; a possible
runners.owner: Dooley Tbreds J Fairhurst Kabin Racing Synd N OHare

runners.name: Enemy
runners.spotlight: Snapped a losing sequence at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) in January before close third at Wolverhampton (2m), and returned to winning ways at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) latest; this 9yo is in a more competitive race today but there remains mileage in his mark judged on his old form; might not be far away.
runners.comment: 9yo who has won two of his last three & there's still mileage in his mark on his old form

runners.name: Charging Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) last July and is now just 1lb higher; however, he hasn't been at the top of his game on his three AW starts since December and needs something extra today.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) last July but not at top of game on AW since December

runners.name: Prydwen
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2024 and he's dropping down the weights; this 8yo has shown the odd sign of spark over the last 12 months but his AW exploits this winter indicate it's best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 but AW exploits this winter indicate it's best to look elsewhere

runners.name: Duke Of Oxford
runners.spotlight: Second in this in 2024 and fourth last year; won at Kempton (2m) in December and followed up by 3l over this C&D in January; this 6yo is on a career-high mark but is clearly thriving and has to be respected.
runners.comment: He's won his last two; up to a career-high mark but clearly thriving; has to be respected
runners.owner: A Cope B Roberts And J Biggane

races.name: BetMGM All-Weather Vase Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Lady Dora Mae
runners.spotlight: Only made her debut in November and bids for a hat-trick after winning a Southwell novice (1m, Tapeta) in January and a four-runner Kempton handicap (7f, Polytrack) by a short head last month; not sure this sharp 7f is completely ideal, but likely there is more to come from her.
runners.comment: Only made her debut in November and bids for a hat-trick; likely there is more to come

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: Has proved most consistent since her debut in October including wins at Chelmsford (5f, Polytrack) in January and here (6f) the following month; should stay this far, but she has an awkward draw.
runners.comment: Most consistent since her debut in October; should stay 7f, but she has an awkward draw

runners.name: Madame De Sevigne
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner on turf, but 0-6 on the AW albeit runner-up on three occasions; hasn't shown much in two starts for this yard, though, and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in two starts for this yard and others make greater appeal

runners.name: So Sassy
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than for her sole success here (6f) in August last year, but her only previous run over this far was inconclusive as regards stamina; visor given a go.
runners.comment: Sole win came over 6f here, but still has stamina to prove; visor on

runners.name: Party Bear
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning form over C&D in January and had genuine excuses for two of her three defeats in the meantime; beaten just a short head and a neck into third of 11 in the Fillies and Mares Trial here in between those efforts with two of these behind; leading player.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head and a neck into third in the F & M trial here; leading player

runners.name: Elizabetty
runners.spotlight: All six AW starts have come at Newcastle, winning a 7f novice there in January; returns from wind surgery but has plenty to prove under these conditions.
runners.comment: Newcastle novice winner back from a wind op; plenty to prove under these conditions
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: Dandy Khan
runners.spotlight: Has nothing to prove under the conditions having won five times over 7f-1m here during the winter, but they were all at Class 6 level and she may again find this sort of company a shade warm.
runners.comment: Won five times here during the winter, but all at Class 6 level; this looks tougher

runners.name: Lady Manzor
runners.spotlight: Not at her best lately including when finishing behind two of these in the Fillies and Mares Trial here in February, but she is a dual C&D winner including when defying stall 14 in this race last year; still 3lb higher, but better drawn this time and cheekpieces are given a go; no great surprise if she figures.
runners.comment: 3lb higher than when defying stall 14 in this race last year; could well figure

runners.name: Handle With Care
runners.spotlight: Has done most of her racing over shorter, but she made all over C&D in December and was beaten just a head by Lady Manzor in this race last year when, like the winner, she was drawn wide; 2lb lower this time around and high on the list.
runners.comment: 2lb lower than when beaten a head by Lady Manzor in this race last year; high on the list

runners.name: Mereside Diva
runners.spotlight: Ended a 16-race losing run in a four-runner contest at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta; first-time blinkers) in February, but could manage only seventh of 11 when sent off 5-2 favourite for the Fillies and Mares Trial over C&D five weeks ago (Party Bear third); needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Only seventh of 11 in the F & M trial over C&D five weeks ago; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Searchingtheblues
runners.spotlight: Landed her first two starts in 7f novices at Kempton (Polytrack) and Wolverhampton (Tapeta) in July 2024, but her form has been patchy in an abbreviated career since; returns from another 74 days off.
runners.comment: Landed her first two starts in July 2024, but her form since has been patchy

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Should appreciate the return to this trip having attempted an extended 1m1f last time, but her recent form has been up and down and the outside stall won't make things any easier.
runners.comment: Recent form has been up and down and the outside stall won't make things any easier

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Vase Marathon Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Shrimp Shady
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who has proved consistent on the AW so far (232441); had only a nose to spare at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) last month, but is just 2lb higher and may still have more to offer upped again in trip; respected.
runners.comment: Has proved consistent on AW so far (232441); up in trip and may still have more to offer

runners.name: Dance Time
runners.spotlight: Mainly consistent on the AW and was only around half a length behind Shrimp Shady when fourth of eight at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) last month, but she finished last of 14 in this race last year which does leave her with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Mainly consistent on the AW, but finished last of 14 in this race last year; bit to prove

runners.name: Tarbat Ness
runners.spotlight: Out-and-out stayer who won twice at Newcastle (2m, Tapeta) at the start of the year, but he looks high enough in the weights now and makes a belated Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: Out-and-out stayer; high enough in the weights now and makes a belated Polytrack debut

runners.name: Selenic
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since her debut early last year, winning at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) and Newcastle (1m2f, Tapeta); steps up another half-mile in trip, but she shapes like a stayer and is worthy of respect, especially if strong in the market; cheekpieces are given a go.
runners.comment: 2-6; steps up another half-mile in trip, but she shapes like a stayer; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Sir F Brooke & Sir R Pilkington

runners.name: Arc Zoosve
runners.spotlight: Saw his race out well when successful at Southwell (2m2f, Tapeta) last month and is 1-1 here, having won over this trip in January; up another 3lb, but still one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January and scored over 2m2f at Southwell last time; one for the shortlist

runners.name: Circuit Breaker
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; 1lb lower than when twice runner-up at Wolverhampton (1m6f/16.5f) during the winter and didn't enjoy much luck when behind a couple of these there last month, but the outside stall won't make things easy.
runners.comment: Didn't enjoy much luck in his latest start, but the outside stall won't make things easy

runners.name: Appier
runners.spotlight: Three course wins have come over 1m4f, but he has won over this sort of trip elsewhere; finished half a length behind St Faz when third of four at Southwell (1m6f, Tapeta) and now 11lb better off; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Half a length behind St Faz at Southwell eight days ago; 11lb better off; each-way claims

runners.name: Charlies Choice
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over 1m4f/1m5f here and Taryn Langley gets on well with him, but he has never attempted this far before and he must put an uncharacteristically poor effort at Newcastle 13 days ago behind him.
runners.comment: Three-time winner over 1m4f/1m5f here, but has never attempted this far before

runners.name: St Faz
runners.spotlight: 3-3 last month including a win over C&D; had only a neck to spare in a four-runner event at Southwell (1m6f, Tapeta) eight days ago and is now 11lb higher including his 5lb penalty, but the ceiling of his ability on the AW is still to be established.
runners.comment: 3-3 last month; 11lb higher, but the ceiling of his ability on AW is yet to be established

runners.name: Francesco Baracca
runners.spotlight: Something of a Wolverhampton specialist (3-5) though he has won on Polytrack at Kempton; the drop to 1m4f didn't help last time so the return to a longer distance should help, but his high draw may not be ideal even over this trip.
runners.comment: Should appreciate stepping back up in distance, but his high draw may not be ideal

runners.name: Diamond Bay
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who has run consistently well in three starts since returned to the AW in October; off four months, but has won twice after longer absences and has dropped 1lb below his last winning mark; a player.
runners.comment: Off four months, but has won twice after longer absences and 1lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Trojan Storm
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, but hasn't beaten many in four starts over 1m-2m on the AW since returning from over five months off in December; slipping back down the weights, but probably best watched for now; stable also runs Vaguely Royal.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many in four starts on the AW since returning in December; best watched

runners.name: Riyami
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger who has become all-or-nothing, suddenly bouncing back to winning form at Dundalk (2m, Polytrack) four weeks ago; 3lb lower than when sixth of 12 in this race two years ago; not quite sure what to expect.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when sixth of 12 in this race two years ago; not quite sure what to expect

runners.name: Vaguely Royal
runners.spotlight: Has dropped a long way in the weights, but is without a win on the Flat since the end of 2023 and has a wide draw to overcome.
runners.comment: Has dropped a long way in the weights, but without a win on the Flat since the end of 2023

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Vase Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Dark Ace
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-5 since being gelded when winning at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) a fortnight ago; he's won twice on Polytrack at Dundalk; this in-form Irish challenger holds solid claims up 4lb for last time.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton a fortnight ago; this in-form Irish challenger holds solid claims
runners.owner: J Fairhurst & Damian McCann & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Carbine Harvester
runners.spotlight: Below best on both runs this year but failed to settle at Kempton last time, when a wide draw also didn't help; twice went close at Kempton (6f) towards the end of last year and this C&D winner is not written off.
runners.comment: Below best on both runs this year but excuses latest; this C&D winner is not written off

runners.name: Justcallmepete
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who won three in a row over 6f on Polytrack elsewhere at the end of last year; some reasonable efforts since but needs to build on those if he's to return to winning ways today; withdrawn at Kempton last month when his nose was stuck in the stall (18-1 at the time).
runners.comment: Won three in a row at the end of 2025; needs to build on this year's performances

runners.name: Accrual
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in January and short-headed here (5f) three days later; however, his subsequent form hasn't come up to scratch; has to raise his game.
runners.comment: C&D win in January; short-headed here three days later; subsequent form not up to scratch

runners.name: Moulin Booj
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (5f, Tapeta) last month and he failed to settle when well beaten from a wide draw over 6f there next time; however, all of his wins have come at 5f and he has something to prove kept to 6f.
runners.comment: Won over 5f at Wolverhampton last month but well beaten over 6f there next time

runners.name: The Thames Boatman
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f here in January and rallied for 1l second of five over 5f here last time; it's possible 5f is his optimum trip nowadays but he won over today's C&D in 2024 and could be in the shake-up if seeing it out.
runners.comment: 2nd over 5f here last time; it's possible 5f is his optimum trip, but could still go well

runners.name: Silky Wilkie
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) in January and he also won at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) in February; he's continued in good form on Tapeta on his last two starts and is also effective over this C&D; might have a part to play.
runners.comment: Effective over C&D and has been running well this year; might have a part to play
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxxi & E Burke

runners.name: Uncle Don
runners.spotlight: 2yo Listed runner-up in September 2024 for Richard Fahey; 0-9 last year but has a dangerous mark to work with on his first start for a new trainer, who can do well with recruits from other yards (remains in the same ownership); interesting, all the more so if attracting market support.
runners.comment: 0-9 last year but has a dangerous mark to work with on first start for a new trainer

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) in November and he's continued in good heart in the main on Tapeta subsequently; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut in November and has continued in good heart in the main; e-w chance

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: 9yo who is inconsistent and hasn't shone on his last three starts but he went close at Kempton (6f) in February and he's finished in the first two on seven of his 11 previous runs on the AW here (including when runner-up in this in 2024); he's well worth a second look now back here.
runners.comment: Inconsistent 9yo; hasn't shone on last few runs but worth a second look given record here

runners.name: Mesaafi
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last June on sole previous course visit and in good form at end of 2025; can be forgiven his run at Newcastle on New Year's Day as he was hampered; could be a contender if fully tuned up after three-month break.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last June on sole previous course visit; had an excuse on last run in January

runners.name: Apache Outlaw
runners.spotlight: Without a win since landing two in a row on the Dundalk Polytrack (6f/7f) in late 2024, and below par there in February last time; however, he's produced some positive performances this winter and has edged back down the weights; might not be far away.
runners.comment: No win since 2024 but some positive runs this winter and has edged back down the weights

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Vase Middle Distance Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: My Fermoy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who managed to defy a three-month absence at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) a fortnight ago; 4lb rise looks manageable and there may yet be more to come from him; considered.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who defied a three-month layoff at Wolverhampton a fortnight ago; considered

runners.name: Wyld Bill
runners.spotlight: Record in AW handicaps for Denis Quinn since last June reads 5112151113, including two wins over C&D; ran right up to his best when third of 11 in an even stronger contest here five weeks ago; major player on first start for John Ryan (same owner) while Jack Dace is 1-1 on him.
runners.comment: Record in AW handicaps since last June reads 5112151113; major player on stable debut

runners.name: Eupator
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who twice made all at Wolverhampton (8.6f/9.5f) in January, but recent evidence suggests the handicapper may have him for the time being.
runners.comment: Twice made all at Wolverhampton in January, but the handicapper may have him now
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner

runners.name: Hitched
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m/1m2f here and just 1lb higher than when beating a dual subsequent winner at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in January, but he has the outside stall and will require some luck given his come-from-behind style.
runners.comment: Four-time course winner; has the outside stall and will require some luck

runners.name: Great Bedwyn
runners.spotlight: Had dropped 3lb below his last winning mark when second of nine behind course-specialist Whitcombe Rockstar at Kempton (1m4f, Polytrack) last month, his first start in 75 days; races off the same mark, but he hasn't tackled a trip this short in three years; stable runs three.
runners.comment: Still 3lb below his last winning mark, but hasn't tackled a trip this short in three years

runners.name: Dark Moon Rising
runners.spotlight: Losing run now up to 14, but his last couple of efforts have been encouraging as when second of six in a slightly stronger contest at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) last month; still on his last winning mark and holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Encouraging efforts in last couple of starts; still on last winning mark; each-way claims

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Dual Chelmsford winner (1m2f, Polytrack) just under a year ago and only 1lb higher than for the latest success; has a bit to find with Wyld Bill and Metallo on February's C&D running, though.
runners.comment: Has a bit to find with Wyld Bill and Metallo on February's C&D running

runners.name: Towerlands
runners.spotlight: Record in AW handicaps reads 11124135; pulled his chance away at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) last month, but may settle better in this bigger field; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Record in AW handicaps reads 11124135; may settle better than last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Penzance
runners.spotlight: Formerly a smart AW performer for Mick Appleby, but not quite the force of old; makes his debut for another new yard (same owners) in a new headgear combination after eight starts for Shark Hanlon; well treated if he could rediscover anything like his best form.
runners.comment: Not the force of old; well treated if he could rediscover anything like his best form

runners.name: Londoner
runners.spotlight: In the frame in six of his eight starts since returning to the AW last September, but his losing run is mounting yet he remains 8lb above his last winning mark; others more compelling.
runners.comment: Losing run mounting up yet remains 8lb above his last winning mark; others more compelling

runners.name: Unterberg
runners.spotlight: 6-11 over 10.7f-1m4f at Dundalk, including winning his last three under Rory Mulligan; 4lb higher mark and different track pose questions, but not opposed lightly.
runners.comment: 6-11 at Dundalk, including winning his last three; new track today but not opposed lightly

runners.name: Metallo
runners.spotlight: 0-11 in handicaps, but is closely matched with Wyld Bill on February's C&D running and ran at least as well as could have been expected when fourth of nine over 7f here a week ago; drawn wider than ideal, but not dismissed entirely.
runners.comment: 0-11 in handicaps, but is closely matched with Wyld Bill on February's C&D running

runners.name: San Andreas
runners.spotlight: Dundalk regular (8-35) who is now at the veteran stage; no faulting his two runner-up efforts over 1m4f/2m this year, but he may be vulnerable to younger legs back down in trip.
runners.comment: Dundalk regular; has run well in both starts this year, but vulnerable to younger legs

runners.name: Night Breeze
runners.spotlight: Remains just 1lb above his last winning mark, but is 0-11 on the AW and has a wide draw to overcome; tongue-tie given a go.
runners.comment: 1lb above last winning mark, but 0-11 on AW and has a wide draw to overcome; tongue-tie on

races.name: BetMGM All-Weather Vase Mile Handicap

runners.name: Unassuming
runners.spotlight: 4lb higher than her last winning mark but Jack Callan's claim will help in that respect and it was a good run last time at Wolverhampton (finished upside Exquisitive Acclaim) given she was never ideally positioned out the back; could go well provided stall 11 isn't a killer.
runners.comment: Latest run can be upgraded and could go well provided stall 11 isn't a killer

runners.name: The Dragon King
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins and one on Tapeta (unraced here); posted career-best RPR when going down narrowly on Newcastle reappearance and got little luck in running when under 3l away at Wolverhampton; unraced beyond 7f but he gives the impression that this far could suit.
runners.comment: Seems in top form; unraced beyond 7f but he gives the impression that this far could suit

runners.name: Talis Evolvere
runners.spotlight: 0-3 here but was runner-up on one of those occasions; returns in a good run of form and only just failed to hang on last month at Newcastle (7f); usually races up the with pace.
runners.comment: 0-3 here but was runner-up the once; returns in a good run of form

runners.name: Ben Lawers
runners.spotlight: Seventh career run when winning a 1m AW maiden at Dundalk which he was entitled to at odds-on; only fifth back there on handicap debut but was never in the best place after starting slowly; may yet to have more to offer.
runners.comment: Recent Dundalk maiden winner; handicap debut back there didn't go to plan

runners.name: I Still Have Faith
runners.spotlight: From a wide draw he did plenty of early running to get his position at Wolverhampton (7f) two weeks ago and that softened him up in the finish; without a win since 2023, but well drawn, stays this far and a tongue-tie is added.
runners.comment: Without a win since 2023, but well drawn, stays this far and a tongue-tie is added
runners.owner: J Morley D Reynolds C Watkins & Partner

runners.name: Angel Hunter
runners.spotlight: He's on a competitive mark and his first run in blinkers last time at Southwell (1m) was better than it looks at first glance; could go well, for all that he's unraced here.
runners.comment: His Southwell run in first-time blinkers was better than it looks at first glance

runners.name: Sous La Neige
runners.spotlight: French raider with a strike-rate of 1-27; while that doesn't bode well, he finished like a train (from stall 11) to finish second in this last year (2lb higher) and he's not been beaten far in his last three races at Deauville.
runners.comment: French raider; fast-finishing second in this last year; has been running well at home

runners.name: Invited
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-11 on the AW and better than ever when a 33-1 third over 1m at Southwell last time out; dual course winner who is well drawn to adopt his prominent style of racing; considered.
runners.comment: Dual course winner who was better than ever when third last time at Southwell

runners.name: Borgi
runners.spotlight: Another prominent racer; now a seven-time winner on the AW after this year's wins over C&D and at Wolverhampton (8.6f); those were Class 4s but he's got a canny habit of never winning by far so remains on a fair mark.
runners.comment: Won his last two; canny habit of never winning by far so remains on a fair mark

runners.name: Exquisite Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner on the Dundalk AW; outran odds of 80-1 when a 2.5l fourth at Wolverhampton (8.6f) but he did benefit from a good position from stall 1 and he's out in 12 this time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Perhaps flattered by his 80-1 fourth last time; has a difficult draw to deal with
runners.owner: Thomas J OConnor & Ms Hazel McGuinness

runners.name: Sir Paul Ramsey
runners.spotlight: Fairly treated on the form he showed in some turf handicaps at the end of last summer, but 0-4 on the AW and his two runs after a break weren't overly encouraging as this represents a tougher test.
runners.comment: Beaten fair and square in two runs after a break and this is no easier

runners.name: Dingle
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning this last year off a 7lb lower mark; dictated steady fractions when winning his penultimate race at Newcastle (1m) and then well held off this rating at Southwell; may need everything to go like clockwork if he's to repeat last year's win off this higher mark.
runners.comment: May need everything to go like clockwork to repeat last year's win off this higher mark

races.name: BetMGM All-Weather Vase Three-Year-Old Handicap

runners.name: Lord Harcourt
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back C&D wins in December and up against it from where he was when a close fifth back here in February; perhaps didn't take a liking to Southwell when below par there last time and could still be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Progressive form at this track; perhaps didn't take a liking to Southwell last time

runners.name: Space Bear
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f for Marco Botti but was effective at 6f, over which he finished second on stable debut here after a Â£26,000 sale; respected off the same mark, for all that he's now into a Class 4.
runners.comment: 7f winner; runner-up over C&D on yard debut; up in grade but has more to offer

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: Won races for his two previous yards, over 6f on turf and Tapeta; twice close up behind Lord Harcourt here in December but finished last at Wolverhampton on stable debut after a break and he's drawn wide here.
runners.comment: Finished last at Wolverhampton on stable debut after a break and he's drawn wide here

runners.name: Alasrae
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-2 in a visor when winning narrowly at Kempton (6f) and kept on well for second over 5f at Wolverhampton despite running in just a tongue-tie (as here); 1lb higher but Jack Callan now takes over with his 5lb claim; considered.
runners.comment: Hard to fault of late and Jack Callan now takes over with his 5lb claim; considered

runners.name: Henrythenate
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running for Stuart Williams with 5f wins here (under Jack Dace) but subsequent defeats suggest he's now high in the weights.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins here for this yard but recent defeats suggest he's high in the weights

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner; had the run of things up front when second over C&D two runs back (some of these behind) and was beaten fair and square next time at Southwell; poorly drawn for one who enjoys being up with the pace.
runners.comment: In a solid enough run of form but poorly drawn for one who enjoys being up with the pace

runners.name: Mister Moet
runners.spotlight: Has won two of his last four races, both here over 6f and 5f; as with stablemate Mighty Vega, he could be vulnerable under a penalty in this higher grade.
runners.comment: As with stablemate Mighty Vega, could be vulnerable under a penalty in this higher grade

runners.name: Numero Vingt
runners.spotlight: All four runs over 6f; dead-heated in a Chelmsford maiden and narrowly defied a penalty here in a novice that's worked out well; made an underwhelming handicap debut at Southwell but he's back at Lingfield with cheekpieces added and well drawn to boot.
runners.comment: Progressive until quiet on handicap at Southwell; better than that and headgear is on

runners.name: U S S Charleston
runners.spotlight: Made it eighth-time lucky as a 2yo in a 5f nursery on Tapeta; not beaten far in 5f/6f handicaps here before an eyecatching third at Wolverhampton where he was dropped in from a wide draw; possibilities.
runners.comment: Has form here; good last run when dropped in from a wide draw at Wolverhampton

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Just the four races for current yard and showed improved form to win his maiden here over 7f; 6f should suit as he can be keen but he finished last over this far at Southwell (off this mark) and did some hanging.
runners.comment: 7f course maiden winner; lesser run over 6f at Southwell and he did some hanging

runners.name: Alvin
runners.spotlight: It was a winnable 5f novice he took at Wolverhampton in February; eased down after a troubled passage on his handicap debut at Southwell (Class 2 over 6f; 11-1) but there's plenty of potential factored in with his current mark.
runners.comment: Excusable defeat on handicap debut but on all his form this looks a tough mark

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: Won his novice over 6f (Polytrack) and added a 7f handicap here last week when well positioned throughout; that was from a wide draw as well but stall 11 spells danger back at 6f, and he's penalised.
runners.comment: Won last week; wide draw as well but stall 11 spells danger back at 6f, and he's penalised

races.name: Midnite All-Weather Vase Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Wonder
runners.spotlight: Running well this year, winning at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta; first-time cheekpieces) in February and going down by a neck in two starts over this C&D either side; up another 1lb, but another big effort is expected.
runners.comment: Running well this year including two neck defeats over C&D; another big effort is expected

runners.name: Tommys Promise
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a four-timer over 1m2f on Polytrack during the winter, including three wins here; has never attempted this far on the Flat before, though, and will need plenty to go right from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Rattled off four-timer during the winter, but has never attempted this far on the Flat

runners.name: Son Of Man
runners.spotlight: Plenty of placed efforts over the past year or so and continues to edge down the weights, but 0-22 since his successful debut and this is a competitive race in which to attempt to regain the winning thread.
runners.comment: Losing run now 22; this is a competitive race in which to try to regain the winning thread

runners.name: Robusto
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; record over C&D reads 1313, but this his first visit here since June 2024; losing run is up to 17 and he is drawn wide; visor the headgear of choice this time.
runners.comment: Record over C&D reads 1313, but losing run is up to 17 and he is drawn wide; visor on

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Bids for a hat-trick after wins over 1m2f at Newcastle (Tapeta) and under Conor Whiteley at Doncaster (good to soft) six days ago; mark has gone up by the same amount as his penalty, so a player if seeing out the longer trip.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick after wins at Newcastle and Doncaster; a player if seeing out 1m4f

runners.name: Damascus Steel
runners.spotlight: In good form for Denis Quinn in recent months including wins at Newcastle (1m4f, Tapeta) in December and over C&D in January; closely matched with Wonder on a couple of bits of form and respected on first start for John Ryan (same owner); stable also runs Tommy's Promise.
runners.comment: In good form in recent months including a win over C&D; respected on stable debut

runners.name: Party Island
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 15, but has won five times over 1m2f-1m4f here and is lower in the weights than when twice runner-up over C&D late last year; cheekpieces removed after one use; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Five course wins; lower in the weights than when twice runner-up over C&D late last year

runners.name: Sea Founder
runners.spotlight: Ran with credit when fourth of 13 at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) 16 days ago having won over 1m2f here the time before, but his only previous go over this far didn't prove his stamina one way or the other.
runners.comment: 1m2f course winner but previous go over 1m4f didn't prove his stamina one way or the other

runners.name: Imperial Decision
runners.spotlight: 0-5, but came closest to winning when beaten a nose over C&D in February; still unexposed, but he could have been better drawn.
runners.comment: Came closest to winning when beaten a nose over C&D in February, but is drawn wide

runners.name: Bystander
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; 13lb below last winning mark, but patchy form in five starts since returning from 14 months off in October; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Patchy form in five starts since returning from 14 months off in October

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Five wins since December, the latest in an amateur riders' event here (1m2f; first-time hood) a week ago; 4lb higher under his penalty, but a bigger concern is the longer trip; engaged 8.18 Southwell Thursday.
runners.comment: Five wins since December, the latest a week ago; longer trip is a concern

runners.name: Sonnerie Power
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who defied a ten-month absence when making a successful stable debut at Kempton (1m3f, Polytrack) in January, but a rise in the weights seems to have found him out twice since.
runners.comment: C&D winner; twice held since making a successful stable debut/reappearance in January

runners.name: Francesi
runners.spotlight: Still 5lb above his last winning mark and all five wins have come over 1m on the AW, including two here; hard to recommend over this trip.
runners.comment: All five wins have come over 1m on the AW; hard to recommend over this trip

runners.name: Bint Al Daar
runners.spotlight: Ended a 19-race losing run at Southwell (1m3f, Tapeta) last month, but couldn't get going in time when fifth of 12 at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) 16 days later; appears to stay this far and holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Ended 19-race losing run at Southwell last month; seems to stay this far; each-way claims

runners.name: Loyal Touch
runners.spotlight: Five wins for the Johnston yard, but has not shown a great deal in six starts at Dundalk for this stable; needs to find more in the new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Not shown a great deal in six starts at Dundalk for this stable; new headgear combination

runners.name: The Bay Warrior
runners.spotlight: Now 4lb lower than when winning over C&D last September, but whether he is running well enough to take advantage is debatable.
runners.comment: 4lb lower than when winning over C&D last September, but not sure he can take adavantage

races.name: Curragh On Course Bookmakers Supporting Free Admission Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: New Yorker
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo/Group 1; RPR 112), sister to useful 5f/6f winner Atletico, half-sister to Australian 7f Group 2 winner Now Or Never, out of unraced half-sister to 6f-7f Group 1 winner Dream Ahead; dam a G1-winning juvenile for this yard and bred to be sharp; first string on jockey bookings; yard's juveniles come on plenty for debuts but a huge player with Moore in the saddle.
runners.comment: Dam a G1 winner at two; first string on jockey bookings; bred to be sharp and huge player
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Lars Soldier
runners.spotlight: 3,500euros yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to prolific/useful 5f winner (inc 2yo) Lord Riddiford; showed promise when beaten 8l into fourth over C&D 19 days ago, sticking to task well having been keen; experience a benefit but up against some potentially smart newcomers.
runners.comment: Promise beaten 8l into fourth over C&D lto; experience a benefit but this is a hot maiden

runners.name: Santo Parvenue
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros yearling; third foal; brother to 7f winner Toy Soldier (inc 2yo; RPR 81); dam 7f AW winner (86), half-sister to winners Lady Camelot (7f 2yo and 2m4f hurdle) and Jocks Wa Hae (1m1f), out of Listed-placed 7f winner; yard does well with 2yos but likely to come on for first run and need further in time.
runners.comment: 7,000euros yearling; brother to 7f winner (RPR 81); likely to need further in time

runners.name: Santorini Storm
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; third foal; brother to Listed-placed 5f/6f winner World Of Darcy (inc 2yo; RPR 90); dam 6f winner (inc AW; 81), out of maiden half-sister to 1m Listed winner Baltic Knight; will need to be well above-average to win this; market can guide.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; brother and dam winners from 5f-6f; bred to be sharp; market check

runners.name: Fiery Anne
runners.spotlight: Equiano filly, half-sister to winners Kendaya (French/Spanish 6f), Iskra One (Polish 6f) and Urban Soul (Greek 7f); dam unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo/1m4f winner, out of 6f 2yo winning half-sister to Irish Derby runner-up Definite Article; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Equiano filly, half-sister to three winners over 6f-7f across Europe; best watched

runners.name: Confucius
runners.spotlight: 1,700,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 7f winner Alfareqa (RPR 89); dam 5f/6f winner (inc 2yo Group 1 Cheveley Park; 115), half-sister to three winners from 7f to 1m4f; expensive purchase and dam G1-winning juvenile; top yard whose juveniles usually come on plenty for experience; one of two similarly excellently-bred types for yard and second string on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Expensive yearling; dam G1 winner at two; second string on jockey bookings; obvious player

runners.name: New Mexico
runners.spotlight: 105,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 6f AW winner (RPR 79), half-sister to Italian 1m winner Blue Saphira, out of 1m winning half-sister to winners Alkior (5f/6f), Flying Hammer (7f/1m) and Rowan Brae (Listed-placed 1m 2yo); second string for yard on jockey bookings; new sire.
runners.comment: Cost 105,000gns as yearling; dam 6f AW winner (RPR 79); second string for yard; new sire
runners.owner: Giselle De Aguiar & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Two Brigade
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army colt, half-brother to five winners inc Catch Cunningham (5f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 87) and Palenville (6f 2yo; 79); dam placed 5f/6f AW (67), half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Etlaala and to dam of outstanding 5f performer Battaash; bred to be precocious and sharp; yard gets first-time-out winners.
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners; dam half-sister to dam of Battaash; bred to be precocious

runners.name: Edward Thatch
runners.spotlight: Cost 70,000euros as a yearling; fifth foal; dam unraced sister to winners Iberia (useful 7f 2yo), World Famous (9.4f) and Perfect Light (8.4f/1m2f), out of 6f Group 3 winning close relative to dam of US 1m Grade 2 winner Giocoso; one of two for a yard that does very well with 2yos; sire had first winner in France last weekend; first string on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Cost 70,000euros as a yearling; dam unraced sister to three winners; first string for yard
runners.owner: Giselle De Aguiar & AMO Racing Limited

races.name: 1xBet.ie Supports Safe Gambling Irish EBF Fillies Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Glory To Be
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly gained a deserved breakthrough win in Navan maiden (5f, soft) in October under this rider, getting up on post; struggled on handicap debut here behind Staysound Susie but met some interference; could go well from 3lb lower on return; cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: First win at Navan (5f, soft) in October; no show on handicap debut here but could go well

runners.name: Rion Rubette
runners.spotlight: Won by 8l off 68 at Limerick last June (7f, good); handicapper took a dim view (18lb rise) and she hasn't been competitive since; may need more respite from the assessor and prefers decent ground.
runners.comment: Harshly treated for impressive Limerick win last year; hasn't recovered since; wants good

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Won 5f Cork maiden last May before being set an ambitious task in the Albany Stakes; change of scenery did the trick as won on debut for this yard 12 days ago at Naas (5f, soft); should stay this far but 7lb rise to overcome.
runners.comment: Cork maiden winner ran at Royal Ascot; won on stable debut at Naas; player up 7lb

runners.name: Joshs Joy
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly at two, sent off fairly short prices for all of her four runs and gained a deserved victory at Naas (6f, yielding) in October when making all; form of her penultimate third at Cork looks very strong; fair mark on handicap debut and receives the 3yo allowance.
runners.comment: Has some strong form in the book; fair mark on handicap debut; receives age allowance

runners.name: Asdana
runners.spotlight: Gained first win at Cork off 67 last August (5f, good) and ended last season with a fine second here behind Staysound Susie (5f, heavy), overcoming a slow start and headed late on; finished last on return last season; market will guide.
runners.comment: Good win at Cork last year and fine 2nd behind one of today's rivals lto; might need this

runners.name: Tai Tam Bay
runners.spotlight: Won 6f Naas handicap off 1lb higher in April 2024; best form last season was on seasonal debut when third over C&D off 78; went close on C&D return last month and fair effort after a slow start at Naas 12 days ago; player despite being 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: Went close on C&D return last month and fair effort after a slow start at Naas
runners.owner: Dan Kiely & Leonard Kinsella & Jennifer Coyle

runners.name: Likedbymike
runners.spotlight: Progressive sprinter last year who knocked on the door in handicaps, collared on post at Cork (6f) and back over 5f at Naas produced with every chance when just held for second; behind Staysound Susie here when last seen but player on return if she's ready to roll; booking of Colin Keane a plus.
runners.comment: Went very close at Cork and Naas last year; behind Staysound Susie when last seen; player
runners.owner: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy

runners.name: Varshini
runners.spotlight: Gowran maiden winner ended last season with two fair efforts in Listed company; tailed off on return when sent off favourite for 7f Naas handicap 12 days ago; cheekpieces are reached for now.
runners.comment: Tailed off on return when sent off favourite for 7f Naas handicap 12 days ago; cheekpieces

runners.name: Staysound Susie
runners.spotlight: Won 5f course handicap (heavy) on final run of 2025 off 8lb lower; needed her seasonal return but eyecatching effort here over 5f six days ago when third of 14, finishing off to good effect; should be bang there for stable in terrific form.
runners.comment: Eyecatching effort here over 5f six days ago, finishing off well; should be bang there

runners.name: Aurora Nova
runners.spotlight: Recorded back-to-back 5f wins at Down Royal and Bellewstown last summer but has been a little too high in the ratings since; back down to last winning mark now and won first time up last term so monitor the betting.
runners.comment: Back down to last winning mark now and won first time up last term so monitor the betting

runners.name: Reimagined
runners.spotlight: Windsor win (6f, good) last July on her second start; stiff task in a Group 3 next time and again well beaten at York on nursery debut; debut for new yard in first-time hood; market will reveal expectations; 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: Windsor win on good last summer; set some stiff tasks after; stable debut; hooded

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 5f on good and yielding ground, including at this venue; struggled in two tries on testing ground, including on return over C&D last month; can only be watched at present.
runners.comment: Struggled in two tries on testing ground, including on return over C&D last month
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M OBrien

runners.name: Gloriously Glam
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf last year over 1m, the latest at Navan off 75 in October (yielding); rounded off the year below her best; she had some good form over this trip as a juvenile so not discounted on return.
runners.comment: She had some good form over this trip as a juvenile so not discounted on return
runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand

runners.name: Bint Majestic Roi
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; 0-7 for Dermot Weld but off the mark on third attempt for this yard when scoring at 50-1 in 1m Dundalk handicap in December; fair fifth from 5lb higher back there next time; not sure to translate that form to turf; 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: Yet to threaten on turf; 50-1 all-weather winner in December; not sure to translate that

runners.name: Goofy Gander
runners.spotlight: Failed to improve from her Galway debut when contesting a 7f maiden at this venue; made late gains once the bird had flown in C&D maiden in October; may prove a different proposition now on handicap debut with good 5lb claimer negating being 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in a couple of maidens; could be a different proposition in handicaps

runners.name: Nancy J
runners.spotlight: Winless since debut Gowran victory (7f, good to firm), albeit highly tried on occasions, running creditably in a hot Royal Ascot handicap last June; didn't see out the mile at Cork when last seen; stable/seasonal debut down in trip; ground a concern.
runners.comment: Won Gowran maiden on fast ground; highly tried since; down in trip for stable debut

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Smullen Series)

runners.name: Chasing Paradise
