runners.spotlight: Showed plenty when 7l second to a potentially smart rival on debut at Leopardstown over 1m on sole start as a 2yo 167 days ago; will improve and bred to appreciate this extra distance; one of the more likely contenders.
runners.comment: Showed plenty on debut when 7l second at Leopardstown; bred to enjoy step up in trip
runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier & Mrs Paul Shanahan & S E Sangster

runners.name: Chablis Rock
runners.spotlight: Improved plenty from debut at Cork when a staying-on third at Gowran over 1m 165 days ago; half-sister to Coltrane, bred to appreciate this extra distance and is in the mix; likely improver.
runners.comment: Improved from debut when third at Gowran 165 days ago; bred to relish this step up in trip

runners.name: Premier Princess
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros yearling; eleventh foal; half-sister to winners Absolutely So (6f/7f inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 113), Mashkoorah (7f AW 2yo; 73), Weekend Getaway (Italian 7.5f-8.7f) and Forbidden Land (Bahraini 7f); unraced dam out of 1m2f Group 2 winner; good pedigree and bred to be effective over shorter; market can guide.
runners.comment: 26,000euros yearling; half-sister to three winners, inc smart 7f performer Absolutely So

runners.name: Stellar Moment
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 88), half-sister to 6f winner Solar Halo, out of 1m 2yo winning close relative of Derby winner Kris Kin; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Ulysses filly; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 88), half-sister to 6f winner; best watched

runners.name: Mistress Of Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; half-sister to six winners inc Earl Of Tyrone (1m4f-2m inc AW/Listed; RPR 112), Countess Of Tyrone (1m4f/2m inc Listed; 95), John Splendid (7f/1m inc 2yo/HK; 84) and Favourite Royal (7f/1m inc AW; 84); unraced dam out of 1m2f Group 1 winner; sharp yard who has done very well with this family; market support needs to be noted.
runners.comment: Newcomer from sharp yard that's done very will with the family; market clues crucial

runners.name: Totally Puzzled
runners.spotlight: 105,000euros yearling; half-sister to four winners inc In Front (French 1m2f-1m6f inc AW/four Listed races; RPR 114); showed ability amid keenness on sole 2yo start when beaten 5.5l into sixth over 1m at Navan; should appreciate this longer trip.
runners.comment: 105,000 yearling; showed ability amid keenness on debut; should enjoy extra trip

runners.name: Sofis Gift
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15.5l into ninth on debut over 1m at Naas 12 days ago; up in trip; another tough assignment.
runners.comment: Beaten 15.5l into ninth on debut at Naas 12 days ago; up in trip; another tough ask

runners.name: Aravalli
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling; half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f AW winner Palazzo Blu (inc 2yo; RPR 99); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (77); well held seventh on debut in 1m maiden at Leopardstown on sole start last season, looking green; off 167 days and up in trip; first-time cheekpieces; should come on plenty but needs to.
runners.comment: Well held in mile maiden on sole start as 2yo, green; should improve for that; up in trip

runners.name: Music And Song
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in two maidens this year on the AW; first-time hood stepping up in trip; makes little appeal.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in two maidens this year on the AW; first-time hood; makes little appeal

runners.name: Arizona Star
runners.spotlight: Showed little in two starts last season to suggest she's capable of winning a maiden; up in trip and more of a long-term project.
runners.comment: Showed little in two starts over 7f last season; more of a long-term project

runners.name: Nathema
runners.spotlight: Shaped as if would improve for debut when beaten 12.5l into eighth at Gowran 165 days ago; yard in excellent form and will benefit from this step up in trip but likely to come up short.
runners.comment: Beaten 12.5l into eighth on debut 165 days ago; yard in excellent form; tough ask however

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Park Express Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Faiyum
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a hugely promising campaign last season, landing a Naas maiden and Killarney conditions race impressively before finishing 1.25l second to subsequent Group 1 winner Barnavara in Naas Group 3; finished off with a smart performance in Group 3 at Gowran over extended 1m1f, powering 7l clear (RPR 114); drop in trip no problem and the one to beat on return.
runners.comment: Hugely promising campaign last term; impressive 7l winner of G3 lto; one to beat on return

runners.name: Princess Child
runners.spotlight: Improved throughout campaign last season, landing competitive handicap at Galway over 7f and following up in Group 3 company at Tipperary before another good effort when fourth in Group 2 at Longchamp; beaten 6l into seventh on return at this track six days ago; should improve for that and step up in trip too; enjoys soft ground and fitness edge; player.
runners.comment: Improver last season; G3 winner, fourth in G2; seventh on return 6 days ago; will improve

runners.name: Moments Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown maiden winner on debut; progressed to be placed in Chesham at Royal Ascot (beaten 2.25l into third) and in a Group 3 over C&D (beaten 2.25l into second); ran with credit when fifth in Fillies' Mile behind stablemate Precise 175 days ago; stable first string and should go well, although soft ground an unknown.
runners.comment: Progressive in four starts last year; last seen fifth in G1 Fillies' Mile; not ran on soft
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: California Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Dual winner on the AW who has ran some cracking races in defeat; standout effort when 2.25l second to Lake Victoria in Irish 1,000 Guineas over C&D in May; didn't go on from that run last season but made a smooth return to action when landing 1m conditions race at Dundalk easily 28 days ago; first-time visor applied; soft ground a worry.
runners.comment: Second in Irish 1,000 Guineas last year; smooth return winning on AW lto; ground a worry
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Catalina Delcarpio
runners.spotlight: Reached a very useful level in three starts last season, landing 1m2f Leopardstown maiden before beaten 2.75l into second in Group 3 at Navan; stepped up in trip and beaten 2-1 favourite when third in the Ribblesdale Stakes at Ascot when last seen 288 days ago; open to further improvement; acts on soft; yard won this last year; trip may be a bit sharp on return.
runners.comment: Beaten fav in Ribblesdale lto on third start; off 288 days; trip on sharp side

runners.name: Bubble Gum
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in French and German Oaks in 2024; Listed winner at Cork for Paddy Twomey last April; below that form when fifth in Munster Oaks on last start 294 days ago; trip may be on the short side on stable debut for Johnny Murtagh.
runners.comment: Listed winner at Cork for Paddy Twomey last year; 294 days off; trip may be too short

runners.name: Cameo
runners.spotlight: Progressive in maidens last season, coping well with testing ground when landing 7f maiden at this track on third run by 2.25l in October; below that level when last seen 164 days ago, beaten 10.5l into fourth in Group 3 as 2-1 favourite; second string for stable.
runners.comment: Maiden winner at this track in soft; below form in G3 164 days ago; second string for yard
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Spicy Margarita
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 14 starts came in Dundalk AW maiden in February of last year; highly tried since, best run over C&D last April when beaten 2.25l into fourth by One Look in this race last year; returned on the AW 28 days ago when beaten 4l into fifth by stablemate California Dreamer; needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Best run came in this race last year when fourth; beaten 4l on return on AW; needs more
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Shes A Nation
runners.spotlight: An impressive maiden winner over C&D in June; coped well with step up in class and far softer ground when 10l third to Faiyum at Gowran Park lto; off 195 days; down in trip; progressive type but likely to struggle to turn the tables with Faiyum off 3lb better terms.
runners.comment: Impressive C&D maiden winner; good run behind Faiyum lto; hard to turn tables with her

runners.name: Celestial Orbit
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner for Ollie Sangster in Britain, including Listed Star Stakes at Sandown in July 2024; last seen 145 days ago finishing 2.5l second to Moon Cloud in Listed mile contest at Saint-Cloud; trainer does very well with recruits, particularly fillies; acts on soft; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Listed winner in Britain; 2.5l second in Listed race in Nov'; yard does well with recruits
runners.owner: J P Magnier/Mrs P Shanahan/Mrs M V Magnier/K MacLennan

races.name: 1xBet.ie Proudly Supporting Irish Racing Mile Handicap

runners.name: Final Voyage
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, last win at Dundalk in February 2024 off 3lb higher; some good efforts last year, primarily on the AW; last seen finishing fourth, beaten 4l by California Dreamer on the AW 28 days ago; probably not particularly well treated.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner; some good efforts on AW in recent months but not well treated

runners.name: Independent Expert
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, sixth turf win last time when beating Quatre Bras by 0.5l at Naas over 7f 12 days ago on second start for new stable; needs to build on that off 4lb higher but could do; acts on all ground but wouldn't want it too soft over a mile.
runners.comment: Landed 7f handicap at Naas 12 days ago; up 4lb and an each-way chance upped in trip

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with easy win in Killarney maiden in October; string of solid efforts in maidens prior to that; beaten 7.75l into sixth over C&D in competitive handicap 152 days ago, shaping better due to traffic problems and being forced wide; interesting each-way contender.
runners.comment: Caught the eye when sixth in C&D handicap final start of last season; interesting

runners.name: Highbury See See
runners.spotlight: Improver in handicaps last season, landed C&D handicap off 6lb lower on final start, beating Great Mover by a head; never involved on return in Irish Lincoln 19 days ago but should come on for that.
runners.comment: C&D winner last season; never involved in Irish Lincoln 19 days ago; should improve

runners.name: Quatre Bras
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW who was out of form on turf last season; enjoyed a revival at Dundalk in recent months and proved as effective on turf when 0.5l second to Independent Expert on soft ground at Naas 12 days ago; step up to a mile suits and usually races off the pace.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner; proved effective on soft when second at Naas lto; trip ideal; chance

runners.name: Daonethatgotaway
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, two on turf, both over a mile on good; in good form on the AW lately, won 1m4f handicap at Dundalk in February; 0.75l second to Unterberg 14 days ago on AW; needs a good pace to aim at and trip a bit sharp; soft ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but needs a good pace to aim at over a sharp trip and ground to dry out

runners.name: Perry Mason
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who has proved very progressive in last 18 months; started winning a handicap at this track off 60 in October before winning impressively at Galway under a penalty six days later and second over C&D week after; benefitted from return run when scoring here six days ago, beating Electric Beauty by a neck; 7lb higher mark but race-fit and should go well.
runners.comment: Progressive, excels over C&D; 7lb up after win here 6 days ago; fitness edge; big player

runners.name: Engines On
runners.spotlight: Very progressive in handicaps last season, two of his three wins coming here over 7f on ground with an ease; latest came in September when beating subsequent winner Tokenomics by 0.75l; may have hit his ceiling judged on return effort in Irish Lincoln (finished 14th) but could easily step forward for that.
runners.comment: Very progressive in handicaps, two wins over 7f at this track; may have hit ceiling

runners.name: Diego El Queso
runners.spotlight: Proved frustrating for much of last season, beaten favourite in three maidens; off the mark at Dundalk in October, beating Ribble Emperor by 3l over a mile; good effort when 1.5l third in conditions race on AW 105 days ago; on an okay mark but soft ground a concern.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Dundalk in October; couple gd runs at same venue; soft ground a concern

runners.name: Vain Gloria
runners.spotlight: Landed first two starts for Paddy Twomey in 2024 at Killarney and Cork over a mile; well-held eighth in Group 3 over 7f on last run; off 697 days and best watched on handicap debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Showed talent, winning two of three starts in '24; off long time; best watched on return

runners.name: Blues Emperor
runners.spotlight: Five wins for Johnny Murtagh, including handicap on Irish Derby day over C&D in July 2023 off 4lb higher; not shown enough in two starts for new outfit to suggest he's well handicapped anymore.
runners.comment: C&D winner who hasn't shown enough in two starts for new yard to think well handicapped

runners.name: Playin Cool
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when fourth and third in a pair of maidens and improved to win over 1m1f on heavy at Leopardstown 166 days ago, beating Shamrock Flame by a neck; form of that questionable; down in trip on handicap debut; acts on good and heavy; yard won this last year.
runners.comment: Improved on third start to win 1m1f maiden at Leop; form nothing special; handicap debut

runners.name: Key Witness
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden winner on the AW for Archie Watson who changed hands for 15,000gns before making a very encouraging stable debut when beaten 0.75l in 6f handicap at this track in October on soft before going one better next time over 7f, beating Miss Nifty by 0.5l; raised 7lb for that but unexposed and trip could easily unlock further improvement; off 152 days; enjoys soft ground.
runners.comment: Second on stable debut over 6f here; won over 7f in November; likes course and soft ground

runners.name: Genuine Article
runners.spotlight: Consistent handicapper last season; last win came at Galway festival over 1m on soft in July; good effort over C&D when short head second in Irish Cambridgeshire in August; acts on all ground; last two hurdle runs of limited significance and should be fitter than most after them.
runners.comment: Consistent handicapper with a few good C&D efforts; fit from hurdle runs; can be involved

runners.name: Iron Fist
runners.spotlight: Pulled 12l clear with next-time-out winner when landing a Killarney maiden in July; excuses next time out and landed an extended 1m1f handicap at Clonmel in good style on latest start 204 days ago; up 7lb but progressive in handicaps and should enjoy conditions; leading player.
runners.comment: Decisive winner of soft ground handicap at Clonmel 204 days ago; up 7lb but progressive

runners.name: Gleneagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on debut at Dundalk in March 2024; 0-11 since but some cracking runs in top handicaps last season, including fifth in Irish Lincoln, third in Victoria Cup at Ascot and second in Ahonoora at Galway; finished off last season with below-par run over 6f but step back up to 1m promises to suit; acts on all ground; a big pot should come his way at some point.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to land a big handicap last season; step up to a mile suits; definite chance

runners.name: Dos Mukasan
runners.spotlight: Latest win on AW over 7f at Chantilly in October; 4-11 in France; not beaten far into 11th in Listed race at Deauville 152 days ago; acts on soft; changed hands for 22,000euros in November; first start for Ger O'Leary.
runners.comment: 4-11 in France; good run in Listed race 152 days ago; acts on soft; stable debut

runners.name: Calm The Jets
runners.spotlight: Landed a mile maiden at Dundalk on debut impressively in January 2024, beating Lady Pimsoll by 2.75l; form worked out okay and gelded since; off a long time and soft ground an unknown; tough ask off opening mark of 84.
runners.comment: Not seen since scoring on debut 448 days ago on AW; gelded since; soft ground an unknown

runners.name: Dar Toungi
runners.spotlight: Useful performer in France, 8-43; latest win at Deauville on the AW in December 2024 over an extened 1m1f; placed in Listed 1m2f race in March last year, third beaten 0.75l; last seen 234 days ago finishing fifth in claimer; acts on soft; first start for Tony Martin off top-weight in competitive handicap; market clues will be interesting.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner in France; placed in Listed 1m2f race in March last year; stable debut

runners.name: Kibris
runners.spotlight: Looked potentially smart when landing C&D maiden in November 2024; didn't reach heights expected in four starts in Group, conditions and handicap company last season but had a couple of good runs over hurdles in recent months; acts on soft and big-field handicap could be right up his street.
runners.comment: Didn't fulfil C&D maiden win promise last season; couple of good runs over hurdles since

runners.name: Westminster Moon
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner in Poland and placed in German Group 1 and Italian Group 2 in 2024; not fired since joining current yard in six starts; down 20lb from rating when first arrived in Ireland but he needs to show more off a 249-day break.
runners.comment: Classy form in Poland, Germany and Italy but failed to fire for this yard yet; needs more

races.name: 1xBet.ie Emerald Cup Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Norwalk Havoc
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner at 7f/1m, including two Listed events at Leopardstown on heavy ground; returned with fair seventh of 25 here in Irish Lincoln behind Ribee; 1lb lower but stamina to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Seventh in Irish Lincoln behind Ribee; 1lb lower but stamina to prove back up in trip

runners.name: Akecheta
runners.spotlight: Gained second heavy-ground win in conditions race at Gowran (7f) last September; yet to win a handicap but sent off the well-backed favourite for the Irish Lincoln on return; slow break cost her any chance; stays this far and folly to dismiss; Colin Keane remains loyal.
runners.comment: Enjoys any ground; slow start when sent off fav' for Irish Lincoln; Keane remains loyal

runners.name: Ribee
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a terrific spell last October, notching three wins and two places during a hectic spell; maintained his rate of improvement when beating 24 rivals here in the Irish Lincoln on return; fine run came to an end in 10.5f Listed at Naas and he's probably better over shorter.
runners.comment: Irish Lincoln winner; fine run came to an end in Listed at Naas & better over shorter

runners.name: Dark Summit
runners.spotlight: Won on good to soft over 1m in France; gelded ahead of Irish debut but outran 80-1 odds in Irish Lincoln when never-nearer eighth of 25; fair chance of improvement up in trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Gelded ahead of Irish debut but outran 80-1 odds in Irish Lincoln; can improve

runners.name: Chica Guerrera
runners.spotlight: Stays 1m4f but dropped back to this trip effectively when a close third over C&D off 70 in October before filling the same spot here over 1m behind Titanium next time from 2lb out of the handicap; Wayne Lordan an eyecatching booking for return.
runners.comment: Big effort over C&D in October; behind Titanium next time; Lordan an eyecatching booking

runners.name: Eastwatch
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 9.5f at Gowran last year (maiden/conditions race); failed to make an impact at Listed level next two starts over further; pulled clear with Ribee when runner-up in Irish Lincoln on return; shortlisted from 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Pulled clear with Ribee when runner-up in Irish Lincoln on return; shortlisted

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: One of four for yard; progressive in handicaps last season, winning three of his last four; commanding win at Listowel in September off 90 (1m3f, heavy); folded tamely when sent off favourite for 1m4f Listed event here in November; will need to be in top order to defy 12lb higher mark on return.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Listowel last year but well beaten fav' in Listed here next time; tough mark

runners.name: Narlita
runners.spotlight: Rewarded for her consistency with a Gowran win in October (1m4f, heavy) before signing off with close-up fourth at Galway; took the Irish Lincoln by the scruff of the neck on return when trying to make all, giving way late to finish fifth; step up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Gowran win in October; cracking effort from the front in Irish Lincoln on return; chance

runners.name: Glen To Glen
runners.spotlight: Fourth runner for yard; won his maiden here over 6f but stayed this far when runner-up over C&D off 90 last July; landed Cork maiden hurdle in December before tailed off in Fred Winter at Cheltenham last month; handicapped to be involved back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Disappointing in the Fred Winter when last seen but handicapped to be involved on Flat

runners.name: Immutable
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle and three-time Flat winner; two tame efforts hurdling in the winter but ended last season with a fair fifth of 16 at Leopardstown over 1m4f off current mark; handles the mud; respected.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose performer handles the mud and has a chance back on the Flat off this mark

runners.name: Fiver Friday
runners.spotlight: Four Flat wins and hurdle victory for Paul Traynor; fine yard debut third over C&D off 1lb higher in October; runner-up in pair of handicap hurdles at Thurles in the winter; stable in form and shortlisted despite being 1lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Good third on C&D stable debut in October; two good runs over hurdles since; shortlisted

runners.name: Mocking
runners.spotlight: Can be a tricky ride but justified market support when notching first win narrowly at Roscommon in October (10.5f, soft); wasn't beaten far in C&D handicap last summer from 2lb higher and ran well on return last season; considered.
runners.comment: Roscommon maiden winner has run well over C&D and goes well fresh; considered

runners.name: Dutch Gold
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in October 2023 but winless since; tailed off in the Irish Lincoln on return and fared no better here over 1m4f six days ago; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Tailed off in the Irish Lincoln on return and fared no better here over 1m4f six days ago
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Coeur dOr
runners.spotlight: Smart sort is only 4-29 but has run well in defeat many times in quality races including when beaten 2.5l into fourth over C&D off 95 in October; this ground could be a bit too testing for him on return but the veteran could still go well.
runners.comment: Smart sort is only 4-29 but has run well in defeat many times in quality races
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P OConnor

runners.name: Tamam Desert
runners.spotlight: Galway maiden winner over 7f at two; highly tried last season, notably in the 1,000 Guineas; kept on well in closing stages when tenth of 25 in Irish Lincoln on handicap bow last month but others preferred.
runners.comment: Tenth of 25 in Irish Lincoln on handicap bow last month but others preferred

runners.name: Bay Colony
runners.spotlight: Highly tried at two; placed over 1m on return last season before landing fillies' maiden at Limerick (1m4f, soft to heavy) in April; never a factor at Listed/Group 1 level after; market will reveal expectations on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Limerick 1m4f maiden winner but tried highly tried after; market will guide on h'cap bow

runners.name: Titanium
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner on turf (including a premier handicap here over 1m in November off 10lb lower); sixth in Irish Lincoln on return when 1lb wrong; race possibly came too soon when below that level over same C&D six days ago.
runners.comment: Won 1m premier handicap here in November; 10lb higher now; player if back to Lincoln form
runners.owner: D C Nolan & D P Kilmurray & Exors of the Late L G & K Clarke

runners.name: Amplitude
runners.spotlight: Rounded off last term in style with an emphatic 6l win in Gowran maiden (9.5f, heavy), seemingly improving for first try on very testing ground; handicapper hasn't taken any chances with his opening mark but front-runner is respected all the same.
runners.comment: Bolted up in Gowran maiden on heavy; handicapper hasn't taken any chance on h'cap debut

runners.name: Comfort Zone
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; three-time hurdle winner went close in 2m4f Ascot Stakes off 93 last June; stayed on for midfield finish in Irish Cesarewitch here in September; finished well when close third in 2m Naas handicap in October; this trip is on the short side.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose performer is a dour stayer on the Flat; this trip looks on the short side

runners.name: Cowardofthecounty
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; debut win here (6f) before going on to taste Group 3 success at Deauville (7f, good) in 2024; tried at Group 1 level after; struggled in three runs last season; tongue-tied ahead of return going up in trip.
runners.comment: 7f Group 3 winner; struggled in three runs last season; tongue-tied ahead of return

races.name: Feel The Rush With 1xBet.ie Easter Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Galileo Noir
runners.spotlight: Weakened into seventh on 1m AW debut at Dundalk in November; improvement down to 7f when third of seven behind a Ballydoyle runner at Naas 12 days ago; stamina to prove over this trip.
runners.comment: Third of seven behind a Ballydoyle runner at Naas 12 days ago; stamina to prove here

runners.name: Pendulum
runners.spotlight: Dubawi colt; dam 1m winner (inc Listed) and third in Irish 1,000 Guineas, sister to 7f 2yo/1m2f winner Antilles, half-sister to smart 9.5f winner Tree Of Knowledge, out of Oaks runner-up Wonder Of Wonders, from family of Galileo; the choice of Ryan Moore; blinkers; of obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Dam third in Irish 1,000 Guineas; the choice of Ryan Moore; blinkers; of obvious appeal
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Atomic City
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath colt cost 170,000gns as a yearling; promising debut third of 20 behind pair of stablemates at this course (1m, heavy) in November; holds Irish Derby entry; one of two for yard, market can separate them.
runners.comment: Expensive purchase was third of 20 here on debut; Irish Derby entry; can improve

runners.name: Glyfada
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly; half-sister to winners Prague (1m/1m2f inc Group 2; RPR 118), Knight Of Honour (1m1f-1m6f inc Bahrain; 95) and Princess Nadia (1m2f; 86); dam 6f 2yo winner (inc Group 3/AW; 110); Irish Oaks entry; considered.
runners.comment: Camelot filly; half-sister to the smart Prague; Irish Oaks entry; considered

runners.name: Canary Island
runners.spotlight: Dubawi colt; half-brother to the top-class Auguste Rodin who won six Group 1s; dam 7f-1m2f winner (inc 2yo/triple Group 1; 119), sister to Magical (1m2f-1m4f Group 1); yard's second string on paper but Irish Derby entry is entitled to maximum respect.
runners.comment: Half-brother to the top-class Auguste Rodin who won six Group 1s; Moore prefers Pendulum
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Blue Blue Moon
runners.spotlight: Homebred daughter of Space Blues; top Moyglare family of Elizabeth Jane and Kyprios; finished fifth in Naas barrier trial before set stiff task on racecourse debut at same venue in Listed grade last July; steps up in trip and encounters softer conditions.
runners.comment: Homebred daughter of Space Blues; tough ask on debut; steps up in trip on softer ground

runners.name: Avoca Woods
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon colt cost 58,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to winners May Night (7f-9.5f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 92) and Sweet Talkin Sue (8.6f AW); dam Swedish 8.6f winner; stable 3-12 in Flat maidens.
runners.comment: Sea The Moon colt cost 58,000euros as a yearling; stable 3-12 in Flat maidens

runners.name: Chamonix
runners.spotlight: Son of Wootton Bassett cost 100,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to several Flat/hurdle winners; dam Group-placed 6f/7f 2yo winner; gelded and tongue-tied ahead of debut; market can separate him and Atomic City.
runners.comment: Son of Wootton Bassett cost 100,000euros; gelded; tongue-tie; market can separate runners

runners.name: Scientific
runners.spotlight: Weak in the betting and ridden by Ryan Moore when well beaten into seventh of 18 on debut here over 1m last month; jockey bookings suggest he's the stable's third string; others preferred.
runners.comment: Didn't show a lot on debut; jockey bookings suggest he's the stable's third string
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

races.name: Your Game. Your Odds. Your 1xBet.ie Stayers Trophy Race

runners.name: Emit
runners.spotlight: Out of the first two only once from six starts last season, going close in a 1m2f Group 3 here in May before a poor run at Ascot; Listed winner at Gowran (1m4f, good) in July before runner-up in Group 2 and Group 3 (both over 1m7f) in France; Ryan Moore booked; gelded ahead of return.
runners.comment: Model of consistency last season, gaining Listed win; Ryan Moore booked; gelded; player

runners.name: Dallas Star
runners.spotlight: Won Leopardstown Group 3 in April 2024 (1m2f, heavy); last win was at Dundalk over a year ago; finished behind A Piece Of Heaven at that venue on latest and might come up a tad short again.
runners.comment: Behind A Piece Of Heaven at Dundalk on latest and might come up a tad short again

runners.name: A Piece Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; three-time Flat winner was second in the 2024 Irish Cesarewitch; has had his issues since but despite being very weak in the betting, ran a lovely second at Dundalk in February (ahead of Dallas Star); entitled to improve but unproven on soft.
runners.comment: Second in the 2024 Irish Cesarewitch; good run at Dundalk latest; unproven on soft

runners.name: Happy Pharoah
runners.spotlight: Already a Leopardstown specialist, winning three times there last season over 1m5f, including a premier handicap in September; never a factor in the Irish Cesarewitch here when last seen; would want drying ground to be considered; gelded.
runners.comment: Leopardstown specialist won three times there last season; needs drying ground; gelded

runners.name: Genealogy
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m for Aidan O'Brien at two; hurdle campaign since joining this yard; chased home the subsequent Fred Winter runner-up Winston Junior in Ascot contest in January; last of five in Naas conditions race behind Saratoga when last seen.
runners.comment: Won over 1m for Aidan O'Brien at two; hard to know what to expect on Flat debut for yard

runners.name: Layfayette
runners.spotlight: Winner of nine races; last of six stakes race wins was in 2023 Royal Whip over 1m2f at this venue; best run last season when runner-up to an odds-on chance in 1m2f Group 3 event here in June; first try at this trip.
runners.comment: Classy sort at his best but overall form is patchy now; first try at this trip

runners.name: Falcon Eight
runners.spotlight: Veteran who got his seventh Flat win at Gowran last April (1m6f, yielding); hasn't counted in four handicaps since and finished last of the 20 runners at Naas when last seen in October; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won at Gowran last April but tailed off in four handicaps since; others preferred
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P OConnor

races.name: Central Bank Ashland Stakes (Grade 1) (Fillies) (Dirt)

runners.name: Percys Bar
runners.spotlight: Has won previously at this course; was on the premises in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies (third, beaten just under 4l) at Del Mar five months ago; returns from a layoff; she's a serious player.
runners.comment: Clear chance; third in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies in October.

runners.name: Nycon
runners.spotlight: Ran encouragingly when just under 4l second (Aqueduct) 34 days ago in an allowance listed; should figure.
runners.comment: Must go on the shortlist; respectable runner-up five weeks ago.

runners.name: Hollybygolly
runners.spotlight: Gained narrow success in an allowance optional claimer at Turfway Park 30 days ago; successful twice in her last three starts; despite that Turfway Park success, she can be taken on.
runners.comment: Despite that Turfway Park success, has questions to answer.
runners.owner: Storyteller Racing Open Gate Horse Ventures LLC Et Al

runners.name: Star Actress
runners.spotlight: Scored narrowly in a maiden special weight when getting off the mark at the second time of asking at Gulfstream Park last month; related to Grade 1 winner Just F Y I and looks the part on breeding (by Justify).
runners.comment: Won by slim margin last month; this daughter of Justify looks the part on breeding.

runners.name: French Friction
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten after two career runs and this daughter of City Of Light looks the part on breeding; easy winner (6l, sloppy track, Oaklawn Park) 48 days ago in a black type; poses a threat.
runners.comment: She can make a bold bid; comfortable winner 48 days ago.

runners.name: Omaha Bay
runners.spotlight: Held in fourth in a Grade 2 upped to 1m at Gulfstream Park 34 days ago; others more likely.
runners.comment: Held in fourth in a Grade 2 upped to 1m at Gulfstream Park 34 days ago; others more likely.

runners.name: Zany
runners.spotlight: Justifying her reputation as a smart prospect in winning all starts to date and this daughter of American Pharoah looks the part on breeding; scored by just under 3l at Tampa Bay Downs two months ago in an allowance listed; no surprise to see her go well.
runners.comment: Likely to be a threat; won by just under 3l two months ago.

races.name: Chairman's Cup By Binaa (Local Grade 1) (Turf)
HM The King's Cup (Group 3) (Turf)
Al Methaq Mile (Listed Race) (Turf)
